# host_system_file (Resource)

Atomically installs one protected regular file at an absolute system path. Managed destinations must be owned by `root`, use a canonical local group name, and have a mode that is not writable by group or other users.

For checksum-checked deletion and atomic replacement to be safe, the complete destination parent chain must already exist, must be root-owned, must not contain symlinks, and must not be writable by group or other users. Use `host_file` instead for files under a user-writable home or temporary directory.

Prefer `source` for scripts and other non-secret repository files. Source bytes are hashed for drift detection but are not stored in Terraform state. Apply refuses to install source bytes that changed after the plan was created. Literal `content` is stored in plaintext state and is not secret-safe.

Existing destinations are rejected unless they are imported or `adopt_existing = true` is set. `delete_on_destroy` defaults to false; when enabled, deletion is refused unless the current content matches the last successfully deployed checksum and the observed destination remains root-owned, non-writable by group or other users, and free of setuid, setgid, and sticky bits.

Privileged commands are resolved from a fixed set of protected system directories rather than the caller's `PATH`. The provider accepts only root-owned, executable utilities that are not writable by group or other users and whose parent chain is protected. File bytes are streamed into root-created staging under the protected destination parent, and sudo authentication is followed by non-interactive privileged commands. This prevents a writable `PATH` or untrusted temporary-file pathname from selecting input that will run through sudo.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "host_system_file" "vpn_up" {
  # Source-backed bytes are hashed but are not copied into Terraform state.
  source      = "${path.module}/vpn-up"
  destination = "/usr/local/bin/vpn-up"

  mode              = "0755"
  delete_on_destroy = true
}
```

## Import

Import an existing file by its absolute destination path:

```shell
terraform import host_system_file.vpn_up /usr/local/bin/vpn-up
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `destination` (String) Clean absolute destination path for the installed regular file.

### Optional

- `adopt_existing` (Boolean) Allow create to take ownership of a pre-existing destination and reconcile it. Defaults to false so existing system files require an explicit import or adoption decision.
- `content` (String) Literal file content. Mutually exclusive with `source`. This value is stored in plaintext Terraform state and must not be used for secrets.
- `delete_on_destroy` (Boolean) Delete the destination on destroy. Defaults to false. When true, deletion is refused if content changed since the last successful install or adoption.
- `group` (String) Canonical destination group name as resolved by the host account database. Defaults to the platform administrator group (`root` on Linux and `wheel` on macOS/FreeBSD).
- `mode` (String) Destination permission mode as four octal digits. Group- or other-writable modes are rejected for privileged files.
- `owner` (String) Destination owner. Privileged system files are restricted to root ownership.
- `source` (String) Local regular file to copy. `~` is expanded with the provider home directory and relative paths are resolved from the Terraform working directory. Mutually exclusive with `content`; source bytes are not stored in state.

### Read-Only

- `checksum_sha256` (String) Observed destination content SHA256. During planning this is the desired source or content SHA256, so source-file changes and destination drift produce a diff.
- `deployed_checksum_sha256` (String) SHA256 last installed or adopted by Terraform. Destructive deletion is refused when the destination no longer matches this checksum.
- `id` (String) Resource identifier, equal to `destination`.
- `source_path` (String) Resolved absolute source path when `source` is configured.
